gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول مضمر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/405)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول مضمر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/405)
text: جَاءَتِ امْرَأَةٌ سَائِلَةٌ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَالِدَاتٌ وَالِهَاتٌ رَحِيمَاتٌ بِأَوْلَادِهِنَّ لَوْ لَا مَا يَأْتِينَ إِلَى أَزْوَاجِهِنَّ لَقِيلَ لَهُنَّ ادْخُلْنَ الْجَنَّةَ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Aban Bin Usman, from Hareyz, from Waleed who said,

‘A woman came over with a question to Rasool-Allah<sup>asws</sup>. So Rasool-Allah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The child bearers, and the kind and merciful with the children, if they do not let (anyone else) come to their husbands (beds), it will be said to them: ‘Enter the Paradise without Reckoning’’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Aban ibn ‘ Uthman from Hariz from Walid who has said the following: “Once a woman came to the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, to ask a question and the Messenger of Allah said, ‘Child bearing, compassionate and kind to their children, had they not brought anyone (in the bed of their husband), it would have been said to them, ‘Enter in paradise without any questions being asked.’”
